articles.title: Moore: American Dream Still Worth Fighting For
articles.summary: Maryland Gov. Wes Moore said patriotism should unite rather than divide Americans, arguing that service and sacrifice—not politics—define love of country and remain essential to fulfilling the nation's founding promise. While speaking with Bloomberg This Weekend hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini Moore also urged leaders to focus on delivering tangible results on affordability, education and economic opportunity, saying voters are looking for action over ideology. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Polls Say American Pride Depends on How You Ask
articles.summary: Between June 8th and 11th, NPR/PBS/Marist conducted a poll of 1,340 adults and found thatAmericans remain broadly proud of their country, but views on patriotism, the nation's future and trust in government are increasingly divided along partisan lines, even as overall levels of national pride have changed little over time. Director at the Eagleton Center for Public Interest at Rutgers Ashley Connan and Puck News writer Abby Livingston join Bloomberg This Weekend and analyze more of the poll data with hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Thousands flock to Tehran to mourn slain Supreme Leader
articles.summary: Thousands flock to Tehran to mourn slain Supreme Leader Thousands of mourners thronged to Tehran to pay their respect to Iran's slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei on Saturday. Sprayed with mists of water to keep cool in temperatures that may nudge 40C in Tehran over the next days, thousands of women and men filled the Grand Mosalla religious complex in Tehran.  "The leader was a father to us all.
